summary: Small assembly section-attribute fix: the PR changed the handwritten assembly declarations for the .dtors and .ctors sections to include writable/allocatable flags, replacing bare `.section .dtors` and `.section .ctors` with `.section .dtors, "wa"` and `.section .ctors, "wa"`. This addressed non-readable segment warnings while preserving the existing labels and data layout.
what_changed: In `asm/MSL/abort_exit.s`, changed the `.dtors` section directive at address range comment `0x803B7260 - 0x803B7280` from `.section .dtors` to `.section .dtors, "wa"`.
In `asm/dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.s`, changed the `.ctors` section directive at address range comment `0x803B7240 - 0x803B7260` from `.section .ctors` to `.section .ctors, "wa"`.
No symbols, labels, instruction bytes, or `.4byte` entries were changed in the provided diff; only section attributes changed.
smart_moves: Fixed the warning at the assembly-source section directive level instead of changing labels or data contents, minimizing risk to matching/layout.
Used explicit section flags `"wa"` for constructor/destructor data sections, making the section writable and allocatable in a way consistent with data-like ctors/dtors tables.
Kept existing address range comments and global labels such as `lbl_803B7260` and `lbl_803B7240`, preserving decompilation traceability.
decomp_lessons: For assembly files that define special data sections such as `.ctors` and `.dtors`, bare `.section` directives may produce segment permission/readability warnings; include the correct section flags when needed.
Metadata-only assembly directive fixes can resolve toolchain/linker warnings without affecting matched code or emitted table entries.
When touching low-level runtime sections, prefer narrowly scoped changes to section attributes rather than restructuring the surrounding startup/exit assembly.
naming_conventions: The PR retained anonymous/decompiler-style labels like `lbl_803B7260` and `lbl_803B7240`; there is no evidence of new symbol naming conventions.
Runtime symbols visible in the diff include `__destroy_global_chain` and `_ExitProcess`, but their names were not changed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `.section .dtors, "wa"` for destructor table data when the table must be writable/allocatable.
Use `.section .ctors, "wa"` for constructor table data when the table must be writable/allocatable.
Keep address comments beside section directives to preserve binary-layout context while adjusting assembler metadata.
follow_up_queries: Find other assembly files with bare `.section` directives for data-like sections that may need explicit flags.
Search for `.ctors` and `.dtors` declarations to verify section attributes are consistent across the project.
Check whether later PRs replaced these assembly sections with C or linker-script definitions and preserved the `"wa"` flags.
